notes.anchor: I mean to Apollo and to Aesculapius
notes.note: Apollo and his son Aesculapius were the chief Greco-Roman gods of healing; Cicero, recovered from illness as he set sail, asks Terentia to make the customary thank-offering to them. He was writing in June 49 BC, leaving Italy to join Pompey against Caesar.

Arpinum, a hill town in Latium some 100 km southeast of Rome, was Cicero's ancestral home and the site of family property. He suggests Terentia retreat there, away from the troops, if grain prices rise in the city.
